slides.title: Money Matters
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 6 English, Unit 5: Money Matters. Welcome to Unit 5: Money Matters. Money is a part of everyday life, but understanding how it works is a skill that takes practice. Whether you are saving coins in a jar at home, choosing between two things you want at the market, or hearing adults talk about bills and budgets, money is always around you. In this unit, you will explore the world of financial literacy — the ability to understand and use money wisely. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand texts about spending, saving, and financial literacy
Use comparative adverbs correctly to compare actions (e.g. *more quickly*, *faster*, *better*)
Use quantifiers to talk about amounts (e.g. *both*, *neither*, *some*, *many*, *a lot of*)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand texts about spending, saving, and financial literacy. you will also use comparative adverbs correctly to compare actions (e.g. *more quickly*, *faster*, *better*). you will also use quantifiers to talk about amounts (e.g. *both*, *neither*, *some*, *many*, *a lot of*). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: humble, eagerly, meager, firmly, sensitive, uncomfortable. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Money Matters: source text 1 and Money Matters: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Comparative Adverbs
bullets: What the rule means and why it matters Comparative adverbs help us compare how actions are done. They tell us if something is done in a greater or lesser...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Comparative Adverbs. What the rule means and why it matters Comparative adverbs help us compare how actions are done. They tell us if something is done in a greater or lesser way than something else. This is important because it helps us describe differences in actions, making our conversations and writing clearer and more interesting. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Writing 1: Opinion Essay on Pocket Money and Saving
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
